title: ranunculus euanthemus
intro: Ranunculus euanthemus, also known as Eastern Woodland Buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family, typically found in the Eastern United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus euanthemus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
